summary: This cluster of research focuses on the taxonomic revision of the diatom genus *Amphora sensu lato*, integrating molecular phylogenetics with detailed morphological analysis to resolve species boundaries and evolutionary relationships. A key finding was the phylogenetic distinction of the subgenus *Halamphora Cleve*, which supported its reclassification as a separate genus, and the identification of *Amphora terroris* as a new species. The studies emphasized that morphological data remain critical for validating molecular-based phylogenies within this group. Geographically, the research spanned diverse marine and estuarine environments, reflecting the global distribution of *Amphora* species.
